dev.jeka.core.api.tooling.docker

Class JkDockerAppTester

  • java.lang.Object
    • dev.jeka.core.api.testing.JkApplicationTester
      • dev.jeka.core.api.tooling.docker.JkDockerAppTester
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    java.lang.Runnable

    public class JkDockerAppTester
extends JkApplicationTester

    • Field Summary

      • Fields inherited from class dev.jeka.core.api.testing.JkApplicationTester

        reAttemptDelay, startTimeout

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Static Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      protected void executeTests()
      Executes the tests for the application under test.
      <T extends JkDockerBuild>
      T      		 getDockerBuild() 
      protected void init()
      Prepares the variables required to start and stop the application, including the base URL and port configuration.
      protected boolean isApplicationReady()
      Check and returns if the application is ready to be tested.
      static JkDockerAppTester of(JkDockerBuild dockerBuild, java.util.function.Consumer<java.lang.String> tester)

      Sets the base URL for the application.
      JkDockerAppTester setContextPath(java.nio.file.Path contextPath) 
      JkDockerAppTester setImageName(java.lang.String imageName) 
      JkDockerAppTester setPort(int port)
      Sets the port for the application to use.
      JkDockerAppTester setShowAppLogs(boolean showAppLogs)
      Configures whether application logs should be displayed during the testing process.
      protected void startApp()
      Starts the application and its environment.
      protected void stopForcefully()
      This method is invoked when, the application did not start until defined timeout.
      protected void stopGracefully()
      This method is invoked when tests are terminated, to undeploy the application and its environment.

      • init

        protected void init()
        Description copied from class: JkApplicationTester
        Prepares the variables required to start and stop the application, including the base URL and port configuration.
        Overrides:
        init in class JkApplicationTester

      • startApp

        protected void startApp()
        Description copied from class: JkApplicationTester
        Starts the application and its environment. This method should be invoked in a separate thread and is expected to block execution. The application should be terminated using the stopXXX methods.
        Specified by:
        startApp in class JkApplicationTester

      • isApplicationReady

        protected boolean isApplicationReady()
        Description copied from class: JkApplicationTester
        Check and returns if the application is ready to be tested.
        Specified by:
        isApplicationReady in class JkApplicationTester

      • executeTests

        protected void executeTests()
        Description copied from class: JkApplicationTester
        Executes the tests for the application under test. The method is supposed to throw an Exception (for instance if tests failed.
        Specified by:
        executeTests in class JkApplicationTester

      • stopGracefully

        protected void stopGracefully()
        Description copied from class: JkApplicationTester
        This method is invoked when tests are terminated, to undeploy the application and its environment.
        Overrides:
        stopGracefully in class JkApplicationTester

      • stopForcefully

        protected void stopForcefully()
        Description copied from class: JkApplicationTester
        This method is invoked when, the application did not start until defined timeout.
        Overrides:
        stopForcefully in class JkApplicationTester

      • setPort

        public JkDockerAppTester setPort(int port)
        Sets the port for the application to use. If left empty, a random port will be choosen.

      • setBaseUrl

        public JkDockerAppTester setBaseUrl(java.lang.String baseUrl)
        Sets the base URL for the application. Default is http://localhost.

      • setShowAppLogs

        public JkDockerAppTester setShowAppLogs(boolean showAppLogs)
        Configures whether application logs should be displayed during the testing process.
